title: Unnamed sockets doesn't work after checkpointing
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Writing to an [unnamed socket pair](https://www.man7.org/linux/man-pages/man2/socketpair.2.html) after checkpoint/restore fails with `EACCES`. It works if using `criu dump`/`criu restore` manually. `--runtime runc` makes no difference. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman run -d ruby:3-slim ruby -r socket -e "a, b = UNIXSocket.pair; loop { a.send('a', 0); p b.recv(1); sleep 1 }"`
2. `podman container checkpoint --latest`
4. `podman container restore --latest`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
-e:1:in `send': Permission denied - send(2) (Errno::EACCES)
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The application to continue running. 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.0
API Version:  3.4.0
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Thu Sep 30 21:40:21 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `criu --version`:**

```
Version: 3.16
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

comments.body:
This means that any Erlang application, that shells out/exec won't be possible to checkpoint (because how erlang solves that without forking the main VM but instead passing arguments to another process that will fork/exec)

```
podman run -d erlang:24-alpine erl -noshell -eval 'L = fun(F) -> io:fwrite(os:cmd("date")), timer:sleep(1000), F(F) end, L(L).'
podman container checkpoint --latest
podman container restore --latest
podman logs -f --latest
```

/kind bug

Is this actually something that Podman can fix?
@adrianreber PTAL

At first I thought it was a bug in `criu`, but manually running `criu dump/restore` (without container), it works as expected, the unnamed socket is restored succesfully and works. I've tried with both `--runtime runc` and `--runtime crun` but it's the same behavior, that's why i reported it to podman.
@carlhoerberg Is this about sockets or erlang? I am confused how it is connected.
Sockets, namely sockets created by https://www.man7.org/linux/man-pages/man2/socketpair.2.html (which Erlang does underneath but that's not important)
Where do you see `-e:1:in `send': Permission denied - send(2) (Errno::EACCES)`? I see that the container dies after restore, but I see no message why it dies.
Could you also give an example how you try it with CRIU only?

Logged to stdout: 

```
# podman run -d ruby:3-slim ruby -r socket -e "a, b = UNIXSocket.pair; loop { a.send('a', 0); p b.recv(1); sleep 1 }"
# podman container checkpoint --latest && podman container restore --latest && podman attach --latest
458d0d54583375fb5cc99f400ebba207319d4b0219fb7ee34c7d9704164276a8
458d0d54583375fb5cc99f400ebba207319d4b0219fb7ee34c7d9704164276a8
-e:1:in `send': Permission denied - send(2) (Errno::EACCES)
        from -e:1:in `block in <main>'
        from -e:1:in `loop'
        from -e:1:in `<main>'
```

With CRIU: 

```
ruby -r socket -e "a, b = UNIXSocket.pair; loop { a.send('a', 0); p b.recv(1); sleep 1 }" &
sudo criu dump -t $(pidof ruby) -vvv -o dump.log --shell-job && echo ok
sudo criu restore -vvvv -o restore.log --shell-job
```
Works if running the container with `--privileged`
And with `--ipc host` it works. Maybe that's expected. Will close this issue.
